title: Web Development Basics
description: Test your understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript fundamentals.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to define an internal style sheet?
questions.explanation: The <style> tag is used to define CSS styles within an HTML document.

questions.question: Which CSS property changes the text color of an element?
questions.explanation: The CSS 'color' property specifies the color of text.

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.

questions.question: How do you select an element with id 'header' in CSS?
questions.explanation: In CSS, the # symbol selects elements by their ID.

questions.question: Which JavaScript method adds an element at the end of an array?
questions.explanation: The push() method adds one or more elements to the end of an array.

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

questions.question: Which property sets the background color of an element?
questions.explanation: The background-color property sets the background color of an element.

questions.question: How do you write a single-line JavaScript comment?
Which HTML attribute adds inline styles to an element?
questions.explanation: The style attribute is used to add inline CSS styling to an HTML element.

questions.question: What does the CSS 'z-index' property control?
questions.explanation: z-index specifies the stack order of elements (which element appears in front or behind others).
